rulings.Case Title: JOSEFINA PACUMIO VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A A BUSINESS ENTITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Judgment Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 4, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SILICON VALLEY BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. MICHAEL B. ROTHENBERG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ***Re-Notice/Amended*** Notice Of Motion And Firsst Amended Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment/Leave To Defend And Quash Service Of Summons; Memo Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declarations; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 4, 2022 line 7. DEFENDANT MICHAEL ROTHENBERG'S NOTIC EOF MOTION AND FIRST AMENDED MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND AND QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS is DENIED. Motion is set for hearing in violation of CCP 1005 and there is no valid proof of service on file. Additionally, moving party failed to carry his burden under CCP 473(b), 473.5 or 473(d). Furthermore, Defendants has been out of possession since June 2022 and the title to the property is no longer held by the Plaintiff. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ROSE WANG VS. ARSEN GABUEV ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 4, 2022 line 10. DEFENDANT ARSEN GABUEV'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED. Motion is set for hearing in violation of CCP 1167.4; Proof of Service for the motion is defective, containing no name of the person purportedly effectuating service. Declaration in support of the motion is insufficient to rebut the presumption afforded to a registered process server. (See Proof of service for the summons and complaint filed on October 17, 2022). Defendant shall file a response to the First Amended Complaint within 5 days of notice of entry of this order if the First Amended Complaint had already been served or per the Code of Civil Procedure if the First Amended Complaint is served on or after November 2, 2022 . The Court notes that there is no proof of service for the First Amended Complaint on file as of November 1, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
